text: Good code is clean code, and clean code is easy to read. When you write code, think about the person who will read it later, even if that person is you. Always use clear naming conventions: a variable called `userAge` is much better than `x`. Break long processes into smaller functions, each with a single purpose. This makes your code easier to test and change later. Well-structured code is also much easier to maintain over time, allowing you to fix problems or add features quickly. Remember, you write code for humans first and computers second.
